regulatory_context.value: Nursing is a regulated-profession route. Destination-country recognition or permission-to-practise applies before work can start, and route compliance cannot be collapsed into a generic training offer.

verified_language_requirements.value: Luxembourg requires language proof and a B2 certificate if no other accepted proof is available; Belgium requires sufficient Dutch, French, or German and explicitly says a simple course-attendance certificate is not enough.

workforce_shortage_context.value: WHO/Europe says the region faces an expected shortage of nearly 1 million health workers by 2030.

value: The legacy exclusivity claim is false; adjacent European models already combine recruitment, language, recognition, and relocation.
The product cannot claim a generic language certificate outcome; Belgium explicitly rejects simple course-attendance proof.
Luxembourg and Belgium route mechanics differ materially and should not be sold as one undifferentiated operational path.
Erasmus+ is only a broad policy funding mention here, not direct proof that this commercial pipeline is eligible.
The £10–17K per nurse cost range is not Benelux-primary-source-safe.

target_audience_summary.value: Recruitment agencies and hospital employers placing foreign-trained nurses into Belgium and Luxembourg, with nurses as the route participants.
target_audience_summary.omission_reason: Reasonable synthesis, but still not a default dashboard truth field.

independent_operator_examples.name: HSE relocation route
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Public analogue proving the route problem and bundled support pattern are real.

independent_operator_examples.name: Charité
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Institutional analogue proving the underlying healthcare mobility context.

independent_operator_examples.name: LiReco
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Public analogue shows the category pattern exists, but not as a clear operator-led search market.

pricing_or_offer_proof: Current proof shows analogue routes, but no clean agency-bundle public pricing layer.
value.social_funnel_signal: Facebook search surfaced active relocation and recruitment communities around `Nursing in Germany`, `Registered Nurses for Germany`, and `Ausbildung and Nursing Job in Germany (Direct Placement in Germany)`. This is a real social-acquisition surface around the route, but the OCR pass did not verify which groups are agency-owned or tied to paid packages.
value.takeaway: The route problem is real, but the visible analogue market is mostly institutional rather than fragmented operators.
value.why_not_stronger: Current evidence confirms analogues, not a target-market operator layer with public paid offers.

independent_operator_summary.omission_reason: Derived independent-operator signal for prioritization and further market research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into product-safe language.

product_certificate_acceptance.omission_reason: The product does not yet identify a route-recognised exam or certificate path.

commercial_model.omission_reason: Desk research does not prove agencies need or will buy this exact bundled service.

pricing_benchmark.omission_reason: Only a weak Irish analogue was located; Benelux pricing evidence is missing.

erasmus_route.omission_reason: Broad policy funding references do not prove this offer fits an actual Erasmus+ application route.

unified_benelux_route.omission_reason: Luxembourg and Belgium language-proof mechanics differ enough that one generic route description is unsafe.
